message.content:
{
  "document_metadata": {
    "board": "CBSE",
    "subject": "Science",
    "class": "IX and X",
    "grade": "9, 10",
    "stream": "",
    "academic_year": "2025-26",
    "total_pages": "13"
  },
  "raw_nodes": [
    {
      "title": "Science Subject Code – 086 Classes IX and X (2025-26)",
      "level": "root",
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "1 SCIENCE Subject Code – 086 Classes IX and X (2025-26) Science Education aims to achieve Scientific understanding of the natural and physical world; Capacities for scientific inquiry; Understanding the evolution of scientific knowledge; Interdisciplinary understanding between science and other curricular areas; Understanding of the relationship between Science, Technology and, Society; Scientific temper and Creativity. The present syllabus has been designed around seven broad themes viz. Food; Materials; The World of the Living; How Things Work; Moving Things, People and Ideas; Natural Phenomenon and Natural Resources."
    },
    {
      "title": "Curricular Goals",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "The Curricular Goals of Science at the Secondary Stage move from the concrete nature of the Middle Stage towards abstraction - from perceptual and practical concepts to theoretical concepts. The Learning Standards (Curricular Goals and Competencies) for Science as an integrated curricular area, in alignment with the National Curriculum Framework 2023 are as follows: CG - 1 Explores the world of matter... C - 7.2 States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well recognised to be inadequate"
    },
    {
      "title": "General Instructions for Assessment",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "It is important to note that the Curricular Goals are interdependent... General Instructions for Assessment: 1. There will be an Annual Examination based on the entire syllabus. 2. The Annual Examination will be of 80 marks and 20 marks weightage shall be for Internal Assessment."
    },
    {
      "title": "Internal Assessment Details",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "For Internal Assessment: i) There will be Periodic Assessment that would include... ii) For 5 marks - Practical / Laboratory work... iii) For 5 marks - Portfolio"
    },
    {
      "title": "COURSE STRUCTURE CLASS IX (2025-26)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Time: 03 Hours Marks: 80 Unit No. Unit Marks I Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour 25 II Organization in the Living World 22 III Motion, Force and Work 27 IV Food; Food Production 06 Total 80 Internal assessment 20 Grand Total 100"
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Materials Unit I",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Matter in Our Surroundings... Atoms and Molecules... Structure of atom..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Formative Content (Class IX)",
      "level": 3,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "The following topics are included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments... Health and Diseases"
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Moving Things Unit III",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Motion... Force and Newton's laws... Gravitation... Floatation... Work, Energy and Power... Sound..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Formative Content (Class IX - Momentum)",
      "level": 3,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "The following topic is included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments... Elementary idea of conservation of Momentum"
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Food Unit IV",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Plant and animal breeding and selection for quality improvement..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Note for Teachers (Class IX)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination."
    },
    {
      "title": "PRACTICALS (Class IX)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Practicals should be conducted alongside the concepts taught in theory classes. LIST OF EXPERIMENTS... Preparation of true solution... suspension... colloidal solution..."
    },
    {
      "title": "COURSE STRUCTURE CLASS X (2025-26)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Time: 03 Hours Marks: 80 Unit No. Unit Marks I Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour 25 II World of Living 25 III Natural Phenomena 12 IV Effects of Current 13 V Natural Resources 05 Total 80 Internal assessment 20 Grand Total 100"
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Materials Unit I (Class X)",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Chemical Reactions and Equations... Acids, Bases and Salts... Metals and Non-metals... Carbon and its Compounds..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: The World of the Living Unit II (Class X)",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Life processes... Control and co-ordination in animals and plants... Reproduction... Heredity and Evolution..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Natural Phenomena Unit III (Class X)",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Reflection of light by curved surfaces... Refraction... Power of a lens... Functioning of a lens in human eye..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: How Things Work Unit IV (Class X)",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Electric current... Ohm's law... Heating effect of electric current... Magnetic effects of current... Direct current. Alternating current..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Theme: Natural Resources Unit V (Class X)",
      "level": 2,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Our environment: Eco-system, Environmental problems, Ozone depletion, waste production and their solutions. Biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances."
    },
    {
      "title": "Note for Teachers (Class X)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book... However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination."
    },
    {
      "title": "PRACTICALS (Class X)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "LIST OF EXPERIMENTS... Finding the pH of samples... Studying properties of acids and bases..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Prescribed Books",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Science-Textbook for class IX-NCERT Publication... Assessment of Practical Skills in Science-Class IX - CBSE Publication..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Question Paper Design (Theory) Class X",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Competencies Total Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding 50 % Application of Knowledge/Concepts 30 % Formulate, Analyze, Evaluate and Create 20 %"
    },
    {
      "title": "Internal Assessment (Class X)",
      "level": 1,
      "parent": null,
      "source_text": "Periodic Assessment - 05 marks + 05 marks... Subject Enrichment (Practical Work) - 05 marks... Portfolio - 05 marks"
    }
  ],
  "classes": [
    {
      "class_name": "IX",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "Annual Examination Structure",
      "raw_nodes": [],
      "units": [
        {
          "unit_id": "U1",
          "unit_name": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour",
          "marks": 25,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Matter in Our Surroundings", "Is Matter Around Us Pure", "Atoms and Molecules"],
          "headings": [],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Definition of matter; Particulate Nature of Matter; States of Matter: solid, liquid and gas",
            "change of state- melting (absorption of heat), freezing, evaporation (cooling by evaporation), condensation, sublimation.",
            "Elements, compounds and mixtures. Heterogeneous and homogenous mixtures, colloids and suspensions.",
            "Physical and chemical changes",
            "Atoms and molecules, Law of Chemical Combination, Chemical formula of common compounds, Atomic and molecular masses.",
            "Structure of atom: Sub-atomic particles; Models of atom; Valency, Atomic Number and Mass Number, Isotopes and Isobars."
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Pure and Impure substances",
            "Separating components (excluded)"
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U2",
          "unit_name": "Organization in the Living World",
          "marks": 22,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Cell - Basic Unit of life"],
          "headings": [],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, multicellular organisms; cell membrane and cell wall",
            "cell organelles: chloroplast, mitochondria, vacuoles, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus",
            "nucleus, chromosomes - basic structure, number.",
            "Tissues, Organs, Organ System, Organism"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Structure and functions of animal tissues (four types)",
            "Meristematic and Permanent tissues in plants"
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U3",
          "unit_name": "Motion, Force and Work",
          "marks": 27,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Motion", "Force and Newton's laws", "Gravitation", "Floatation", "Work, Energy and Power", "Sound"],
          "headings": [],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Distance and displacement, velocity; uniform and non-uniform motion along a straight line; acceleration",
            "distance-time and velocity-time graphs for uniform motion and uniformly accelerated motion",
            "Newton's Laws of Motion, Action and Reaction forces, Inertia of a body, Momentum",
            "Universal Law of Gravitation, Force of Gravitation of the earth (gravity), Acceleration due to Gravity; Mass and Weight; Free fall.",
            "Thrust and Pressure. Archimedes' Principle; Buoyancy.",
            "Work done by a Force, Energy, power; Kinetic and Potential energy",
            "Nature of sound and its propagation in various media, speed of sound, range of hearing in humans"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "uniform circular motion (elementary idea)",
            "Lawof conservation of energy (excluding commercial unit)"
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U4",
          "unit_name": "Food; Food Production",
          "marks": 6,
          "explicit_chapters": [],
          "headings": [],
          "possible_chapters": ["Plant and animal breeding"],
          "topics": [
            "Plant and animal breeding and selection for quality improvement and management",
            "Use of fertilizersand manures; Protection from pests and diseases; Organic farming."
          ],
          "subtopics": [],
          "key_concepts": []
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "X",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "Annual Examination Structure",
      "raw_nodes": [],
      "units": [
        {
          "unit_id": "U1",
          "unit_name": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour",
          "marks": 25,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Chemical Reactions and Equations", "Acids, Bases and Salts", "Metals and Non-metals", "Carbon and its Compounds"],
          "headings": [],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation, endothermic exothermic reactions, oxidation and reduction.",
            "definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH– ions; identification using indicators",
            "concept of pH scale (Definition relating to logarithm not required), importance of pH in everyday life",
            "preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris.",
            "Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds",
            "Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention.",
            "Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon",
            "Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Ethanol and Ethanoic acid (only properties and uses), soaps and detergents."
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U2",
          "unit_name": "World of Living",
          "marks": 25,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Life processes", "Control and co-ordination in animals and plants", "Reproduction", "Heredity and Evolution"],
          "headings": [],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "'Living Being'. Basic concept of nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals.",
            "Tropic movements in plants; Introduction of plant hormones",
            "Nervous system; Voluntary, involuntary and reflex action; Chemical co-ordination: animal hormones.",
            "Reproduction in animals and plants (asexual and sexual) reproductive health - need and methods of family planning. Safe sex vs HIV/AIDS."
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Mendel's contribution- Laws for inheritance of traits",
            "Sex determination; brief introduction"
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U3",
          "unit_name": "Natural Phenomena",
          "marks": 12,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Reflection of light by curved surfaces", "Refraction"],
          "headings": [],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Images formed by spherical mirrors, centre of curvature, principal axis, principal focus, focal length, mirror formula (Derivation not required), magnification.",
            "Laws of refraction, refractive index. Refraction of light by spherical lens; Image formed by spherical lenses",
            "Lens formula (Derivation not required); Magnification. Power of a lens.",
            "Functioning of a lens in human eye, defects of vision and their corrections"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Refraction of light through a prism, dispersion of light, scattering of light",
            "applications in daily life (excluding colour of the sun at sunrise and sunset)"
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U4",
          "unit_name": "Effects of Current",
          "marks": 13,
          "explicit_chapters": ["Electric current", "Magnetic effects of current"],
          "headings": [],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Ohm's law; Resistance, Resistivity, Factors on which the resistance of a conductor depends.",
            "Series combination of resistors, parallel combination of resistors and its applications in daily life.",
            "Heating effect of electric current and its applications in daily life. Electric power",
            "Magnetic field, field lines, field due to a current carrying conductor"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Fleming's Left Hand Rule, Direct current. Alternating current: frequency of AC.",
            "Advantage of AC over DC. Domestic electric circuits."
          ],
          "key_concepts": []
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U5",
          "unit_name": "Natural Resources",
          "marks": 5,
          "explicit_chapters": [],
          "headings": [],
          "possible_chapters": ["Our environment"],
          "topics": [
            "Eco-system, Environmental problems, Ozone depletion, waste production and their solutions.",
            "Biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances."
          ],
          "subtopics": [],
          "key_concepts": []
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "formative_only_content": [
    {
      "title": "Health and Diseases (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "The following topics are included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments. The reduces academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning... Health and its failure. Infectious and Non-infectious diseases, their causes and manifestation. Diseases caused by microbes (Virus, Bacteria and Protozoans) and their prevention; Principles of treatment and prevention. Pulse Polio programmes.",
      "related_unit": "Organization in the Living World",
      "related_chapter": "",
      "assessment_status": "formative"
    },
    {
      "title": "Elementary idea of conservation of Momentum (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "The following topic is included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments. The reduces academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning... Elementary idea of conservation of Momentum",
      "related_unit": "Motion, Force and Work",
      "related_chapter": "",
      "assessment_status": "formative"
    }
  ],
  "practicals": [
    {
      "title": "Preparation of solutions (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "1. Preparation of: Unit-I a) a true solution of common salt, sugar and alum b) a suspension of soil, chalk powder and fine sand in water c) a colloidal solution of starch in water and egg albumin/milk in water",
      "related_unit": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Preparation of mixture/compound (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "2. Preparation of Unit-I a) A mixture b) A compound using iron filings and sulphur powder",
      "related_unit": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Physical or chemical changes reactions (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "3. Perform the following reactions... Iron with copper sulphate solution; Burning of magnesium ribbon in air; Zinc with dilute sulphuric acid...",
      "related_unit": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Stained temporary mounts (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "4. Preparation of stained temporary mounts of (a) onion peel, (b) human cheek cells & to record observations and draw their labeled diagrams",
      "related_unit": "Organization in the Living World (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Identification of tissues (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "5. Identification of Parenchyma, Collenchyma and Sclerenchyma tissues in plants... from prepared slides.",
      "related_unit": "Organization in the Living World (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Melting point/Boiling point determination (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "6. Determination of the melting point of ice and the boiling point of water.",
      "related_unit": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Laws of reflection of sound (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "7. Verification of the laws of reflection of sound.",
      "related_unit": "Motion, Force and Work (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Density determination (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "8. Determination of the density of solid (denser than water) by using a spring balance and a measuring cylinder.",
      "related_unit": "Motion, Force and Work (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Loss in weight/Archimedes principle (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "9. Establishing the relation between the loss in weight of a solid when fully immersed... with the weight of water displaced by it",
      "related_unit": "Motion, Force and Work (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Speed of pulse propagation (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "10. Determination of the speed of a pulse propagated through a stretched string/ slinky",
      "related_unit": "Motion, Force and Work (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Conservation of mass (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "11. Verification of the law of conservation of mass in a chemical reaction.",
      "related_unit": "Motion, Force and Work (Unit III) / Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Finding pH samples (Class X)",
      "source_text": "1. A. Finding the pH of the following samples by using pH paper/universal indicator: Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, NaOH solution...",
      "related_unit": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Properties of acids and bases reactions (Class X)",
      "source_text": "2. Studying the properties of acids and bases... on the basis of their reaction with Litmus solution, Zinc metal...",
      "related_unit": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Reactions classification (Class X)",
      "source_text": "3. Performing and observing the following reactions... Action of water on quicklime; Action of heat on ferrous sulphate crystals...",
      "related_unit": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Reactivity series experiment (Class X)",
      "source_text": "4. Observing the action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al metals on salt solutions... Arranging in decreasing order of reactivity",
      "related_unit": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Ohm's Law verification (Class X)",
      "source_text": "5. Studying the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I)... plotting a graph between V and I.",
      "related_unit": "Effects of Current (Unit IV)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Equivalent resistance (Class X)",
      "source_text": "6. Determination of the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel.",
      "related_unit": "Effects of Current (Unit IV)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Stomata observation (Class X)",
      "source_text": "7. Preparing a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.",
      "related_unit": "World of Living (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "CO2 respiration experiment (Class X)",
      "source_text": "8. Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.",
      "related_unit": "World of Living (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Acetic acid properties (Class X)",
      "source_text": "9. Study of the following properties of acetic acid: Odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus...",
      "related_unit": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Soap cleaning capacity (Class X)",
      "source_text": "10. Study of the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water.",
      "related_unit": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour (Unit I)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Focal length determination (Class X)",
      "source_text": "11. Determination of the focal length of: Concave mirror, Convex lens by obtaining the image of a distant object.",
      "related_unit": "Natural Phenomena (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Light path through glass slab (Class X)",
      "source_text": "12. Tracing the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab...",
      "related_unit": "Natural Phenomena (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Binary fission/Budding study (Class X)",
      "source_text": "13. Studying (a) binary fission in Amoeba, and (b) budding in yeast and Hydra with the help of prepared slides.",
      "related_unit": "World of Living (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Light path through prism (Class X)",
      "source_text": "14. Tracing the path of the rays of light through a glass prism.",
      "related_unit": "Natural Phenomena (Unit III)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Embryo identification (Class X)",
      "source_text": "15. Identification of the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed (pea, gram or red kidney bean).",
      "related_unit": "World of Living (Unit II)",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "activities": [],
  "projects": [
    {
      "title": "Portfolio Assessment Component",
      "source_text": "For Internal Assessment: iii) For 5 marks - Portfolio that includes classwork and other sample of student's work.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "assessment_information": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Annual Examination (Theory): 80 Marks",
      "Internal Assessment: 20 Marks",
      "Grand Total: 100 Marks",
      "Class IX Unit I: 25 marks, II: 22 marks, III: 27 marks, IV: 6 marks",
      "Class X Unit I: 25 marks, II: 25 marks, III: 12 marks, IV: 13 marks, V: 5 marks",
      "Internal Assessment Breakdown (Theory): Periodic Assessment - 05 marks + 05 marks; Subject Enrichment (Practical Work) - 05 marks; Portfolio - 05 marks"
    ],
    "excluded_or_non_assessed_content": [
      "Separating the components of a mixture",
      "Commercial unit of Energy in conservation of energy",
      "Definition relating to logarithm for pH scale (Class X)",
      "Derivation of mirror formula and lens formula",
      "Colour of the sun at sunrise and sunset",
      "Information presented in NCERT text book boxes"
    ],
    "teacher_notes": [
      "The present syllabus has been designed around seven broad themes viz. Food; Materials; The World of the Living; How Things Work; Moving Things, People and Ideas; Natural Phenomenon and Natural Resources.",
      "Curricular Goals are interdependent, and not separate curricular pieces of study.",
      "Attainment of competencies shall be done through transaction of the curriculum using appropriate pedagogy; these shall be assessed through an integrated evaluation scheme.",
      "The following topics are included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments. The reduces academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning. Schools can integrate these with existing chapters as they align well.",
      "Relevant NCERT textual material is enclosed for reference regarding formative content (Health and Diseases, Conservation of Momentum).",
      "The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination.",
      "Practicals should be conducted alongside the concepts taught in theory classes."
    ]
  }
}
